message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Hide Self from @Mention Autocomplete

## Context

Users can currently see themselves in the @mention autocomplete dropdown when typing `@` in comments, replies, and opinions. Since mentioning yourself serves no purpose (backend already filters self-mentions from generating notifications), the current user should be excluded from the suggestion list entirely.

## Files to modify

1. **`client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js`** — accept optional `excludeUserId` param, filter results
2. **`client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js`** — pass `excludeUserId` through to `useMentionAutocomplete`
3. **`client/src/components/comments/comments.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`
4. **`client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`
5. **`client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/OpinionsEditor.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`

## Changes

### 1. `useMentionAutocomplete.js`
- Add `excludeUserId` as 3rd parameter
- After fetching results, filter: `res.data.filter(u => u.id !== excludeUserId)`

### 2. `useTextareaMention.js`
- Add `excludeUserId` as 5th parameter
- Pass it through: `useMentionAutocomplete(value, cursorPos, excludeUserId)`

### 3–5. All three consumers
- Each already has `const { user } = useAuth()` or equivalent
- Pass `user?.id` as the new last argument to `useTextareaMention()`

## Verification

1. Log in and type `@` followed by your own username in a comment/reply
2. Confirm you do NOT appear in the dropdown
3. Confirm other matching users still appear normally


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\2ea23c00-72a1-43b0-a3b0-5a6affda1516.jsonl

planContent:
# Hide Self from @Mention Autocomplete

## Context

Users can currently see themselves in the @mention autocomplete dropdown when typing `@` in comments, replies, and opinions. Since mentioning yourself serves no purpose (backend already filters self-mentions from generating notifications), the current user should be excluded from the suggestion list entirely.

## Files to modify

1. **`client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js`** — accept optional `excludeUserId` param, filter results
2. **`client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js`** — pass `excludeUserId` through to `useMentionAutocomplete`
3. **`client/src/components/comments/comments.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`
4. **`client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`
5. **`client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/OpinionsEditor.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`

## Changes

### 1. `useMentionAutocomplete.js`
- Add `excludeUserId` as 3rd parameter
- After fetching results, filter: `res.data.filter(u => u.id !== excludeUserId)`

### 2. `useTextareaMention.js`
- Add `excludeUserId` as 5th parameter
- Pass it through: `useMentionAutocomplete(value, cursorPos, excludeUserId)`

### 3–5. All three consumers
- Each already has `const { user } = useAuth()` or equivalent
- Pass `user?.id` as the new last argument to `useTextareaMention()`

## Verification

1. Log in and type `@` followed by your own username in a comment/reply
2. Confirm you do NOT appear in the dropdown
3. Confirm other matching users still appear normally

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of the changes:

1. **`useMentionAutocomplete.js`** — Added `excludeUserId` parameter; filters it out of search results before displaying
2. **`useTextareaMention.js`** — Added `excludeUserId` parameter and passes it through to `useMentionAutocomplete`
3. **`comments.jsx`** — Passes `user?.userData?.[0]?.id` as `excludeUserId`
4. **`commentsCards.jsx`** — Passes the existing `userId` variable as `excludeUserId`
5. **`OpinionsEditor.jsx`** — Destructured `user` from `useAuth()` (previously only had `session`) and passes `user?.userData?.[0]?.id` as `excludeUserId`

The current user will no longer appear in @mention autocomplete across comments, replies, and opinions.

file.content:
# Hide Self from @Mention Autocomplete

## Context

Users can currently see themselves in the @mention autocomplete dropdown when typing `@` in comments, replies, and opinions. Since mentioning yourself serves no purpose (backend already filters self-mentions from generating notifications), the current user should be excluded from the suggestion list entirely.

## Files to modify

1. **`client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js`** — accept optional `excludeUserId` param, filter results
2. **`client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js`** — pass `excludeUserId` through to `useMentionAutocomplete`
3. **`client/src/components/comments/comments.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`
4. **`client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`
5. **`client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/OpinionsEditor.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`

## Changes

### 1. `useMentionAutocomplete.js`
- Add `excludeUserId` as 3rd parameter
- After fetching results, filter: `res.data.filter(u => u.id !== excludeUserId)`

### 2. `useTextareaMention.js`
- Add `excludeUserId` as 5th parameter
- Pass it through: `useMentionAutocomplete(value, cursorPos, excludeUserId)`

### 3–5. All three consumers
- Each already has `const { user } = useAuth()` or equivalent
- Pass `user?.id` as the new last argument to `useTextareaMention()`

## Verification

1. Log in and type `@` followed by your own username in a comment/reply
2. Confirm you do NOT appear in the dropdown
3. Confirm other matching users still appear normally

` tag? Any parsing of @mentions? 2. In `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/` — how are opinions rendered? Look for opinion card/list components that display opinion text. Check if @mentions are parsed there. 3. Look at `helpers/handleClicks.js` for `handleClickProfile` — how does profile navigation work? What params does it need? 4. Check if there's any existing utility for parsing @mentions in displayed text (search for "mention" "parseMention" "renderMention" "linkifyMention" patterns in client/src/). 5. Check the `/@username` route pattern — how does visiting a profile by username work? Look at router config. Be thorough — search for any existing mention-rendering utilities or components.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report on my findings. ## Summary: @Mentions Rendering & Profile Navigation Analysis Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's what I found: --- ### 1. **Comments Rendering (cm-body) - PLAIN TEXT, NO MENTION PARSING** **File:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` (line 188) ```jsx

{comments?.comment}

``` **Current State:** Comments are displayed as **plain text only**. There is **NO parsing or rendering** of @mentions in comment display. If a comment contains `@john`, it will display literally as text without being converted to a clickable link or styled mention. --- ### 2. **Opinions Rendering - PLAIN TEXT, NO MENTION PARSING** **Files:** - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/SidebarOpinions.jsx` (line 141) - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/opinionViewer.jsx` (line 100) ```jsx // In SidebarOpinions.jsx
{opinionsData.opinion}
// In opinionViewer.jsx
{opinion.opinion}
``` **Current State:** Opinions are also displayed as **plain text only** with no @mention parsing or rendering. --- ### 3. **Profile Navigation - handleClickProfile Function** **File:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/helpers/handleClicks.js` (lines 77-95) ```javascript export const handleClickProfile = (navigate) => { return(e, loggedInUserId, clickedUserId, clickedUsername) =>{ if(loggedInUserId === clickedUserId){ navigate('/profile'); } else if(clickedUsername){ navigate(`/u/${clickedUsername}`, { state: { userId: clickedUserId } }) } else { navigate(`/visitProfile?userId=${clickedUserId}`, { state: { userId: clickedUserId } }) } } } ``` **Parameters:** - `loggedInUserId` - ID of currently logged-in user - `clickedUserId` - ID of profile being visited - `clickedUsername` - (Optional) The @username for the newer /@username route format **Behavior:** - If clicking own profile → navigates to `/profile` - If username available → navigates to `/u/{username}` (newer pattern) - Fallback → navigates to `/visitProfile?userId={id}` (legacy) --- ### 4. **@Username Route Pattern - FULLY IMPLEMENTED** **File:** `/c/Users/johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\App.jsx` (lines 122-127) ```jsx }> }/> }/> }/> }/> ``` **Implementation in Visitprofile.jsx** (lines 30-50): - Accepts `:username` param from URL - Fetches user data via `getUserByUsername()` API endpoint - Falls back to legacy `/visitProfile?userId=` pattern if no username available - Stores `userId` in navigation state for backward compatibility --- ### 5. **Existing @Mention Infrastructure - Editor-Only** **Mention creation happens ONLY in the editor:** - **Location:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionNode.jsx` - **Type:** Lexical DecoratorNode with MentionComponent - **Stores:** `mentionName`, `mentionUserId`, `mentionUsername` - **Display in Editor:** Rendered as `@{mentionName}` styled chip - **Serialization:** Exported as JSON with all mention metadata **Mention UI Components (Editor-focused):** - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionComponent.jsx` - Clickable mention chip (navigates to profile if not editable) - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/mentions/MentionDropdown.jsx` - Autocomplete dropdown for selecting mentions - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js` - Hook for textarea-based mention input (comments) - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js` - Search/filter users for mention selection **MentionPlugin (Editor):** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/Plugins/MentionPlugin.jsx` - Detects `@` pattern in Lexical editor - Creates MentionNode when user is selected from dropdown - Stores mention with username for later rendering --- ### 6. **Gap Analysis - NO Utilities for Displaying @Mentions in Plain Text** **Searched locations:** - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/utils/` - No mention-parsing utilities exist - Full grep search for `parseMention`, `renderMention`, `linkifyMention` - **ZERO matches** - Comment/opinion text is stored as plain strings with no special encoding **Current flow:** 1. Editor: User creates mention via MentionPlugin → stored as Lexical JSON 2. API: Mention data extracted from Lexical JSON and stored as `comment`/`opinion` plain text 3. Display: Comments/opinions rendered as plain `

{text}

` - no parsing happens --- ### Key Takeaways for Implementation: 1. **Comments & Opinions Need Parsing:** You'll need to create a utility function to parse plain text strings and detect `@username` patterns, then convert them to clickable profile links. 2. **Use Existing handleClickProfile:** When rendering mentions, use the existing `handleClickProfile(navigate)` function - it already handles username-based navigation correctly. 3. **Mention Data Available:** MentionNode stores `mentionUsername` which can be used to navigate via the new `/@username` routes. 4. **Storage Gap:** Comments/opinions store plain text. If you want to preserve rich mention metadata, you'd need to either: - Store Lexical JSON in the backend (like posts do) - Create a separate `mentions` table linking comments to mentioned users - Parse and extract username references from plain text at runtime 5. **CSS/Styling Ready:** The `.mention-chip` styles in the editor suggest styling infrastructure exists that can be adapted for displayed mentions.

Excellent! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report on my findings. ## Summary: @Mentions Rendering & Profile Navigation Analysis Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's what I found: --- ### 1. **Comments Rendering (cm-body) - PLAIN TEXT, NO MENTION PARSING** **File:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` (line 188) ```jsx

{comments?.comment}

``` **Current State:** Comments are displayed as **plain text only**. There is **NO parsing or rendering** of @mentions in comment display. If a comment contains `@john`, it will display literally as text without being converted to a clickable link or styled mention. --- ### 2. **Opinions Rendering - PLAIN TEXT, NO MENTION PARSING** **Files:** - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/SidebarOpinions.jsx` (line 141) - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/opinionViewer.jsx` (line 100) ```jsx // In SidebarOpinions.jsx
{opinionsData.opinion}
// In opinionViewer.jsx
{opinion.opinion}
``` **Current State:** Opinions are also displayed as **plain text only** with no @mention parsing or rendering. --- ### 3. **Profile Navigation - handleClickProfile Function** **File:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/helpers/handleClicks.js` (lines 77-95) ```javascript export const handleClickProfile = (navigate) => { return(e, loggedInUserId, clickedUserId, clickedUsername) =>{ if(loggedInUserId === clickedUserId){ navigate('/profile'); } else if(clickedUsername){ navigate(`/u/${clickedUsername}`, { state: { userId: clickedUserId } }) } else { navigate(`/visitProfile?userId=${clickedUserId}`, { state: { userId: clickedUserId } }) } } } ``` **Parameters:** - `loggedInUserId` - ID of currently logged-in user - `clickedUserId` - ID of profile being visited - `clickedUsername` - (Optional) The @username for the newer /@username route format **Behavior:** - If clicking own profile → navigates to `/profile` - If username available → navigates to `/u/{username}` (newer pattern) - Fallback → navigates to `/visitProfile?userId={id}` (legacy) --- ### 4. **@Username Route Pattern - FULLY IMPLEMENTED** **File:** `/c/Users/johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\App.jsx` (lines 122-127) ```jsx }> }/> }/> }/> }/> ``` **Implementation in Visitprofile.jsx** (lines 30-50): - Accepts `:username` param from URL - Fetches user data via `getUserByUsername()` API endpoint - Falls back to legacy `/visitProfile?userId=` pattern if no username available - Stores `userId` in navigation state for backward compatibility --- ### 5. **Existing @Mention Infrastructure - Editor-Only** **Mention creation happens ONLY in the editor:** - **Location:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionNode.jsx` - **Type:** Lexical DecoratorNode with MentionComponent - **Stores:** `mentionName`, `mentionUserId`, `mentionUsername` - **Display in Editor:** Rendered as `@{mentionName}` styled chip - **Serialization:** Exported as JSON with all mention metadata **Mention UI Components (Editor-focused):** - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionComponent.jsx` - Clickable mention chip (navigates to profile if not editable) - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/mentions/MentionDropdown.jsx` - Autocomplete dropdown for selecting mentions - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js` - Hook for textarea-based mention input (comments) - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js` - Search/filter users for mention selection **MentionPlugin (Editor):** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/Plugins/MentionPlugin.jsx` - Detects `@` pattern in Lexical editor - Creates MentionNode when user is selected from dropdown - Stores mention with username for later rendering --- ### 6. **Gap Analysis - NO Utilities for Displaying @Mentions in Plain Text** **Searched locations:** - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/utils/` - No mention-parsing utilities exist - Full grep search for `parseMention`, `renderMention`, `linkifyMention` - **ZERO matches** - Comment/opinion text is stored as plain strings with no special encoding **Current flow:** 1. Editor: User creates mention via MentionPlugin → stored as Lexical JSON 2. API: Mention data extracted from Lexical JSON and stored as `comment`/`opinion` plain text 3. Display: Comments/opinions rendered as plain `

{text}

` - no parsing happens --- ### Key Takeaways for Implementation: 1. **Comments & Opinions Need Parsing:** You'll need to create a utility function to parse plain text strings and detect `@username` patterns, then convert them to clickable profile links. 2. **Use Existing handleClickProfile:** When rendering mentions, use the existing `handleClickProfile(navigate)` function - it already handles username-based navigation correctly. 3. **Mention Data Available:** MentionNode stores `mentionUsername` which can be used to navigate via the new `/@username` routes. 4. **Storage Gap:** Comments/opinions store plain text. If you want to preserve rich mention metadata, you'd need to either: - Store Lexical JSON in the backend (like posts do) - Create a separate `mentions` table linking comments to mentioned users - Parse and extract username references from plain text at runtime 5. **CSS/Styling Ready:** The `.mention-chip` styles in the editor suggest styling infrastructure exists that can be adapted for displayed mentions.

1. Comments/opinions store `@username` as plain text
2. There's NO existing utility to parse and render @mentions as clickable links
3. `handleClickProfile` already handles navigation by username
4. The `/u/:username` route is fully set up

I need to understand: when a user types `@loren-the-great` via the mention autocomplete and submits, what gets stored? Just the plain text `@loren-the-great`? Let me check the mention insertion logic in useTextareaMention.js - yes, from the code I already read:


When a mention is inserted, it stores the plain text `@username` directly. So I can parse `@username` patterns from the stored text.

The challenge is that I only have the username, not the userId. The `/u/:username` route can fetch the user by username, so `handleClickProfile` with `clickedUsername` should work. But looking at the function signature, it expects both `clickedUserId` and `clickedUsername` for the self-check logic.

Since I don't have the userId from the parsed text, the simplest approach is to create a utility that parses `@username` mentions and replaces them with clickable spans that navigate directly to `/u/username`. For self-detection, I can just compare the mentioned username against the current user's username.

input.content:
# Make @Mentions Clickable in Comments & Opinions ## Context When users type `@username` via the mention autocomplete in comments, replies, and opinions, the text is stored as plain text (e.g. `"great post @loren-the-great!"`). Currently it renders as static text in a `

` tag with no interactivity. Users expect `@mentions` to be clickable links that navigate to the mentioned user's profile. ## Approach Create a shared `MentionText` component that parses plain text for `@username` patterns, rendering them as clickable styled spans. Clicking navigates to `/u/{username}` (or `/profile` if it's the current user's own username). ## Files to create ### 1. `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.jsx` (new) - Accepts `text` prop (the raw comment/opinion string) - Regex-splits text on `@([\w-]+)` pattern - For each `@username` match, render a `` that on click: - If `username === currentUser.username` → `navigate('/profile')` - Else → `navigate('/u/{username}')` - Uses `useAuth()` for current user's username, `useNavigate()` for routing - Non-mention text segments render as plain text - Stops event propagation on click (parent containers have their own click handlers) ### 2. `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.css` (new) - `.mention-link` — styled as clickable: color accent, cursor pointer, font-weight 500, hover underline ## Files to modify Replace raw `{text}` with `` in these 7 locations: | # | File | Line | Current | Change to | |---|------|------|---------|-----------| | 1 | `comments/commentsCards.jsx` | 188 | `{comments?.comment}` | `` | | 2 | `SidebarOpinions/SidebarOpinions.jsx` | 141 | `{opinionsData.opinion}` | `` | | 3 | `SidebarOpinions/visitedProfileOpinions.jsx` | 122 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 4 | `SidebarOpinions/OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` | 111 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 5 | `SidebarOpinions/opinionViewer.jsx` | 100 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 6 | `SidebarOpinions/MyOpinions.jsx` | 90 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 7 | `SidebarOpinions/OpinionssPage.jsx` | 276 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | Each file gets an `import MentionText from '../mentions/MentionText'` (path adjusted per location). ## Verification 1. Post a comment containing `@someuser` via the mention autocomplete 2. Confirm the `@someuser` text appears styled/colored differently from surrounding text 3. Click it → should navigate to `/u/someuser` 4. Type your own `@username` in a comment → click it → should navigate to `/profile` 5. Check opinions in sidebar, opinion viewer, visited profile opinions, and opinions page all render clickable mentions too 6. Confirm clicking a mention doesn't also trigger the parent container's click handler (e.g. opening opinion viewer)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Hide Self from @Mention Autocomplete

## Context

Users can currently see themselves in the @mention autocomplete dropdown when typing `@` in comments, replies, and opinions. Since mentioning yourself serves no purpose (backend already filters self-mentions from generating notifications), the current user should be excluded from the suggestion list entirely.

## Files to modify

1. **`client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js`** — accept optional `excludeUserId` param, filter results
2. **`client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js`** — pass `excludeUserId` through to `useMentionAutocomplete`
3. **`client/src/components/comments/comments.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`
4. **`client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`
5. **`client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/OpinionsEditor.jsx`** — pass `user?.id` as `excludeUserId`

## Changes

### 1. `useMentionAutocomplete.js`
- Add `excludeUserId` as 3rd parameter
- After fetching results, filter: `res.data.filter(u => u.id !== excludeUserId)`

### 2. `useTextareaMention.js`
- Add `excludeUserId` as 5th parameter
- Pass it through: `useMentionAutocomplete(value, cursorPos, excludeUserId)`

### 3–5. All three consumers
- Each already has `const { user } = useAuth()` or equivalent
- Pass `user?.id` as the new last argument to `useTextareaMention()`

## Verification

1. Log in and type `@` followed by your own username in a comment/reply
2. Confirm you do NOT appear in the dropdown
3. Confirm other matching users still appear normally
